_metadata.description: Ingredients backed by clinical research and studies - Enhanced for Section C v3.4.0
changelog: 2.3 (2026-02-17): Evidence-quality cleanup pass removed 6 weak/inconclusive entries, renamed PRECLIN_PEA -> INGR_PEA, and downgraded study_type for low-certainty nutrient/herbal records. Total entries: 164
2.2 (2026-02-17): Added 33 clinically reviewed ingredient records (branded, generic, and emerging), expanded elderberry alias coverage for exact-match evidence mapping. Total entries: 170
2.1 (2026-01-05): Added 12 new clinically-backed branded ingredients (Nitrosigine, LJ100, Shoden, Testofen, Magtein, Sunfiber, Cynatine HNS, TamaFlex, HMB, Zynamite, HEAL9). Total entries: 137
2.0 (2025-12-04): Added study_type field support, updated evidence_level mapping for hierarchy scoring

evidence_level_values.product-human: Exact product tested in human RCTs (1.0x multiplier)
evidence_level_values.branded-rct: Branded ingredient with own RCTs (0.8x multiplier) - e.g., KSM-66, Meriva

study_type_values.systematic_review_meta: Meta-analysis or systematic review (6 base pts)
study_type_values.clinical_strain: Probiotic strain clinical evidence (4 base pts)
study_type_values.in_vitro: In vitro/cell studies only (1 base pt)

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple human placebo-controlled trials for arthritis, inflammation, bioavailability.
Published trials for stress/anxiety, libido, muscle, memory; full-spectrum root extract.
Standardized leaf/root extract; trials in stress, sleep, cognition.
Curcumin with turmeric oil; multiple RCTs in inflammation, depression, joint pain.
Nanoparticle curcumin; high absorption. Trials on arthritis, inflammation, cognition.
Used in multiple hospital and international studies for malnutrition support.
Uses BCM-95; positive evidence for inflammatory conditions.
Multiple clinical studies support use for circulation, skin, ADHD, blood pressure.
Backed for antibiotic-associated diarrhea, gut infections, IBS.
Natural astaxanthin from H. pluvialis; multiple clinical studies for eye strain, skin photoprotection, and exercise recovery.
Clinically tested for spore-based gut microbiome support.
Major clinical trials in heart failure, statin users; preferred bioactive CoQ10.
Buffered vitamin C; clinical evidence for improved bioavailability.
Plant extract used in numerous plant-based clinical trials.
German-manufactured creatine with 99.95% purity. Extensive research in strength, power, cognitive function.
Patented amino acid chelated minerals with superior bioavailability studies.
Fourth-generation folate with clinical studies in pregnancy, cardiovascular health.
European-approved 2mg prolonged-release melatonin with extensive sleep studies.
Clinically studied lactoferrin for iron deficiency and immune support.
Pharmaceutical-grade L-carnitine with studies in exercise performance, heart health.
Organic kelp extract with natural iodine; studies in thyroid function.
Clinical studies in memory, cognitive function, stress response, exercise recovery.
Australian pharmaceutical-grade extract; clinical studies in liver function.
Unique mitochondrial-targeted antioxidant. Human RCT in vascular, cellular stress.
Memory, cognition, neuroprotection in elderly and stroke patients.
Green tea amino acid, calming effect; validated anxiolytic in studies.
Spore-forming probiotic with clinical studies in digestive health and IBS.
Branded lutein/zeaxanthin blend with clinical eye health studies.
Baker's yeast beta-glucan with multiple immune function RCTs.
Patented black pepper extract proven to enhance absorption of nutrients and herbs.
Tripeptide form of glutathione with clinical studies showing increased blood glutathione levels.
Distilled MSM with clinical studies in joint health and exercise recovery.
Multi-mineral complex from marine algae with bone and joint health studies.
Patented solid lipid formulation; multiple human studies on brain health, mood, and superior absorption compared to standard curcumin.
Standardized to 95% curcuminoids; over 80 clinical trials on conditions like arthritis, metabolic syndrome, and liver health.
Patented low-dose collagen; RCTs show efficacy in osteoarthritis and rheumatoid arthritis, better than glucosamine/chondroitin combos.
Matrix of collagen II, chondroitin, and HA; human trials for skin aging and joint support.
Patented NR precursor; multiple human RCTs on NAD+ boosting, safety, and benefits for aging and muscle.
Natural MK-7 form; clinical trials on osteoporosis prevention and vascular health.
Standardized to Lepticrosalides; RCTs in adolescents and adults for mood and sleep disorders.
Proprietary blend; clinical studies on stress reduction and weight control in stressed individuals.
Standardized extract; extensive trials for age-related cognitive decline and peripheral circulation.
Patented urolithin A; human trials on mitochondrial health and physical performance in aging.
Well-studied for high-intensity exercise; increases carnosine levels in muscles.
Human trials support use for endurance, blood pressure, and erectile function.

Strong 2024-2025 systematic review evidence from multiple meta-analyses supports hydrolyzed collagen peptides for skin (hydration, elasticity, wrinkles) and joint health. Low molecular weight peptides (<6 kDa) offer superior absorption. Benefits typically after 90 days.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2024-2025 systematic review of 26 RCTs (n=1,721): hydrolyzed collagen significantly improved skin hydration (Z=4.94, P<0.00001) and elasticity (Z=4.49, P<0.00001). 2025 review of type I collagen (n=1,652, studies 2011-2024) showed positive outcomes for joints.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Oral supplementation shown in trials to improve knee osteoarthritis and skin health.
Strong evidence from trials for hypertension and lipid profile improvement.
Human studies confirm roles in preventing arterial stiffness and osteoporosis.
Trials support use in thyroid disorders and as an antioxidant.
Human trials for depression, fibromyalgia, and weight loss via serotonin modulation.
Comparable to prescription antidepressants in trials; also effective for osteoarthritis.
Extensively studied for cognitive enhancement, fat burning, and athletic performance.
Extensively studied for blood sugar control, lipid management, weight management.
Cocoa flavanol with studies in muscle growth, cardiovascular health, exercise performance.
Standardized rhodiola extract with consistent clinical results in fatigue and stress.

2024-2025 evidence supports magnesium bisglycinate for sleep quality and anxiety. August 2025 RCT confirms modest but significant improvements with excellent tolerability. Superior GI tolerance vs magnesium oxide.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: August 2025 RCT (n=155) showed magnesium bisglycinate (250mg Mg + 1523mg glycine daily) significantly reduced Insomnia Severity Index with effect size d=0.2. 2024 systematic review of 15 studies found benefits for anxiety and sleep.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Extensive systematic review evidence supports NAC as powerful antioxidant and glutathione precursor. Well-established for respiratory health (mucolytic, COPD), liver protection, and emerging psychiatric applications. Modulates glutamate pathways.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Multiple systematic reviews support NAC for glutathione enhancement, liver support, and respiratory health. Strong evidence for COPD, chronic bronchitis, acetaminophen overdose. RCTs demonstrate benefits for psychiatric conditions including bipolar, depression, OCD, and addiction.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human studies show anti-inflammatory and antiviral effects; often studied in combination with zinc or vitamin C.
Shown to reduce stress and improve focus in healthy adults; synergistic with caffeine.
Improves memory and attention in healthy adults and elderly; standardized extracts used in trials.

Strong 2024 evidence supports CoQ10 (ubiquinone preferred over ubiquinol) for heart failure with significant mortality and hospitalization reductions. Improves ATP production and mitochondrial function. Effective for statin-induced myopathy.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: October 2024 BMC meta-analysis of 33 RCTs: CoQ10 reduced all-cause mortality (RR=0.64, P=0.002) and HF hospitalization (RR=0.50, P<0.00001). July 2024 review found increased ATP (SMD 0.82, P<0.001) and mitochondrial capacity (SMD 0.75, P<0.001).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Extensive systematic review evidence supports omega-3 for cardiovascular health, triglyceride reduction, and mood. High-dose EPA particularly effective for CV risk reduction. Quality and formulation affect bioavailability.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: REDUCE-IT trial showed high-dose EPA (4g/day icosapent ethyl) significantly reduced cardiovascular events. Multiple systematic reviews confirm EPA/DHA reduce triglycerides and support cardiovascular health. RCTs support mood benefits.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Extensive evidence from systematic reviews and meta-analyses (2024-2025) supports vitamin D3 for bone health, immune function, and mood. 2024 Endocrine Society guidelines recommend empiric supplementation for specific populations.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2024 DO-HEALTH RCT showed modest benefit on total hip BMD with 2000 IU daily. 2024 Endocrine Society guidelines recommend for children 1-18 years to reduce URTIs, pregnant women, prediabetic patients, and adults 75+ for mortality.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong 2024 evidence shows zinc lozenges (>75mg/day elemental zinc) substantially reduce cold duration when started within 24 hours. Zinc picolinate form shows good bioavailability and tolerance.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2024 analysis showed zinc lozenges (>75mg/day) shortened common cold duration by 33-37% (P<10^-7). Zinc acetate and gluconate lozenges both effective. Meta-analysis showed 2.25 days reduction. Start within 24 hours of onset.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human studies support macular pigment density and visual performance improvements.

Moderate evidence from case series and open-label trials support biotin for hair and nail health, particularly in deficiency states. Limited placebo-controlled RCT evidence. Vitamin B7 essential for fatty acid synthesis. High doses (>10mg) may interfere with lab tests.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Used in trials for hair loss and brittle nails with moderate evidence in deficiency states. Limited high-quality RCT evidence in non-deficient individuals. Some open-label studies show improvements in hair and nail quality.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong systematic review evidence supports melatonin for sleep onset latency, jet lag, and circadian rhythm disorders. Multiple meta-analyses confirm dose-dependent effects. Particularly effective for jet lag, shift work, delayed sleep phase. Generally safe short-term.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Multiple systematic reviews and meta-analyses confirm melatonin effectiveness for sleep onset, jet lag, and circadian rhythm disorders. Strong evidence for shift work disorder and delayed sleep phase syndrome. Dose-dependent: 0.5-5mg typically effective. Prolonged-release (Circadin) benefits insomnia in adults 55+.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Improves mental performance, immune function, and reduces fatigue in human studies.
Shown to reduce stress-related fatigue and improve mood in multiple clinical trials.
2024-2025 systematic reviews confirm B12 essential for nerve and blood cell health. Clear benefits in deficiency and subclinical deficiency. Methylcobalamin and adenosylcobalamin are active forms.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2025 Annals of Neurology study found B12 levels associated with CNS injury biomarkers in older adults. 2025 systematic review on neurological sequelae included 10 RCTs. Clear benefits in deficiency states.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 2024-2025 systematic reviews strongly support Lactobacillus and Bifidobacterium for gut health and immune function. Improved intestinal barrier, microbiota modulation, SCFA production. Strain-specific effects: L. acidophilus and B. lactis most successful.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2024-2025 systematic reviews show probiotics significantly boost Bifidobacterium (SMD 1.85, P=0.01) and Lactobacillus (SMD 2.22, P=0.02). 2024 study showed Lactobacillus-based probiotics decolonized pathogens (62% vs 53%, P=0.04). Benefits for IBS with B. breve, B. longum, L. acidophilus.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong 2024-2025 systematic review evidence supports curcumin for anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. Meta-analyses confirm inflammatory marker reductions. Moderate quality evidence for OA pain relief. Bioavailability major limitation; enhanced formulations show >100-fold improvement.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2024-2025 systematic reviews/meta-analyses (66 RCTs): curcumin significantly reduces CRP (WMD -0.58 mg/L), TNF-α (WMD -3.48 pg/mL), IL-6 (WMD -1.31 pg/mL). Recent meta-analysis in prediabetes/T2D found reductions in inflammatory markers. Greater benefits ≥1g/day.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 2024-2025 systematic reviews confirm vitamin C's role in immune function, antioxidant defense, and collagen synthesis. Enhanced forms (calcium ascorbate, liposomal) show improved tolerance. Shortens cold duration.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2025 systematic review in Nutrients found calcium ascorbate EC superior to ascorbic acid for tolerability and leukocyte concentrations. 2024 review confirms vitamin C reduces cold duration, greater effects on severe symptoms.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human trials show benefits for nausea, joint pain, and anti-inflammatory effects.

Strong 2024-2025 systematic review evidence supports ashwagandha for stress and anxiety reduction. Consistently reduces cortisol through HPA axis modulation. Multiple meta-analyses confirm anxiety scale benefits. Generic extract effective at 250-600mg daily.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: January 2025 systematic review: ashwagandha significantly reduced cortisol (-1.16 μg/dL, P<0.001). 2024 meta-analysis of 9 RCTs (n=558): significant improvements in PSS (MD=-4.72), Hamilton Anxiety (MD=-2.19), cortisol (MD=-2.58). Dose 250-600mg/day for 4-13 weeks.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human studies show metabolic benefits, antioxidant effects, and fat oxidation enhancement.
Clinical studies support liver protection and enzyme normalization.
Human trials show cognitive improvements and nerve growth factor stimulation in elderly with mild cognitive impairment.
Clinical trials demonstrate improved sleep quality and reduced sleep onset time through thermoregulatory effects.
Natural source of melatonin with human studies showing improved sleep duration and quality.

Multiple RCTs and meta-analyses demonstrate elderberry (Sambucus nigra) reduces cold and flu duration/severity. 2021 review confirms safety with no cytokine storm concerns. Antiviral effects via immune stimulation. Start at symptom onset. Added plain "elderberry" alias for exact-match coverage.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2021 BMC systematic review of 5 RCTs (n=180) found elderberry reduces cold/flu duration and severity with large effect sizes. 2019 meta-analysis showed substantial reduction in upper respiratory symptoms. May be comparable to oseltamivir with fewer side effects.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human studies show blood glucose reduction and testosterone support in men.
Traditional medicine with human studies supporting blood glucose management.
Multiple RCTs for diabetic neuropathy, blood sugar control, and antioxidant effects.
FDA-approved heart health claim; extensive evidence for cholesterol reduction.
Contains natural statins; multiple RCTs for cholesterol management.
Multiple RCTs for osteoarthritis; prescription form available in Europe.
Clinical evidence for joint health; often combined with glucosamine.
Multiple RCTs for urinary tract health and UTI prevention.
Multiple RCTs showing blood glucose improvements in type 2 diabetes.
Clinical evidence for digestive support and pancreatic insufficiency.
Prebiotic fiber with RCTs for gut microbiome and mineral absorption.

2023-2025 systematic review evidence supports ferrous bisglycinate as superior chelated iron with better absorption and GI tolerance than ferrous sulfate. Meta-analysis confirms hemoglobin and ferritin increases with fewer adverse events, particularly in pregnancy.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2023 systematic review/meta-analysis: ferrous bisglycinate shows benefit over other iron supplements for hemoglobin and reducing GI adverse events in pregnant women. 2025 study: bisglycinate reversed iron deficiency in 14 days without GI inflammation (unlike ferrous sulfate).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: 2024-2025 evidence shows 5-MTHF is bioavailable active folate form avoiding MTHFR gene concerns and reducing unmetabolized folic acid. Multiple RCTs confirm equivalence to folic acid with lower UMFA. Beneficial for MTHFR polymorphisms. Growing support for prenatal use.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: January 2024 RCT (British Journal of Nutrition): 5-MTHF as effective as folic acid for maternal folate while reducing unmetabolized folic acid by ~50%. 2024 reviews support for pregnancy. Both low and high maternal folate associated with increased CHD risk (U-shaped curve).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Multiple RCTs for sleep improvement; traditional use validated.
RCTs showing anxiety reduction and sleep quality improvement.
Human studies show mood improvement and cognitive enhancement.
Clears senescent cells in animal models, extending healthspan; emerging human interest.
Induces autophagy and extends lifespan in yeast, flies, and mice; mechanistic rationale for aging.
Rejuvenates epigenome and extends lifespan in mice; potential for metabolic disorders.
Inhibits CD38 enzyme to boost NAD+; preclinical data for sleep and anti-aging effects.
Stabilizes mast cells and reduces neuroinflammation in models; potential for allergies and brain health.
Extends lifespan in worms and mice; supports mitochondrial health mechanistically.
Improved absorption form; preclinical senolytic effects with dasatinib.
Direct NAD+ precursor; mechanistic support for fatigue and cognition, limited human data.
Methyl donor in animal models; potential for homocysteine reduction and osmoregulation.
Carotenoid with strong antioxidant mechanisms; branded forms have human data, generic lacks specific trials.
Strong mechanistic data for Nrf2 activation and phase II detox enzymes; limited human trials.
Shown to improve cognition and reduce oxidative stress in animal models; human data sparse.
Supports bile flow and cholesterol metabolism in vitro; limited human efficacy data.
Protects gastric mucosa and supports ulcer healing in rodent models; human trials lacking.
Supports gut lining integrity and mucosal repair; limited human data outside Japan.
Inhibits 5-LOX and leukotriene synthesis; human trials inconsistent or underpowered.
Acetylcholinesterase inhibitor with strong animal data; human trials limited and regional.
Shown to improve ATP production and oxygen utilization in rodents; human data sparse.
Used to support stomach acid; mechanistic rationale exists but no formal human trials.
Neuroprotective and retinal effects shown in rodents; human trials emerging but not definitive.
Shown to improve insulin signaling in vitro and in rodents; human trials inconclusive.
Enhances mitochondrial function and neuroprotection in preclinical models; some small human trials but more data needed.
Boosts NAD+ levels and mitochondrial function in animal models; human trials are ongoing but limited.
Supports mitochondrial biogenesis in preclinical models; limited human data.
Strong preclinical data for longevity pathways; human trials show mixed results.
Strong preclinical anti-inflammatory data; bioavailability issues limit human efficacy without enhancement.
Strong mechanistic data; HCl form has human evidence but generic forms lack specific studies.
60+ clinical studies. Meta-analysis confirmed efficacy for periodontitis. World's most studied probiotic for oral health.
30+ studies including 8 clinical trials. RCTs show 28-35% improvement in cognitive tests (TMT-B). 1.5g dose comparable to 8g citrulline malate for vasodilation. Patented by Nutrition21.
25+ human clinical studies. MIT/Malaysian government research collaboration. RCTs show 16.4% higher testosterone, 32.3% lower cortisol. Standardized to 40% glycosaponins, 22% eurypeptides.
High-potency extract (35% withanolide glycosides). 2024 RCT showed significant cortisol reduction and testosterone increase. 60-120mg effective dose. 72% sleep improvement vs 29% placebo.
Multiple RCTs: 8-week study (n=138) showed greater strength gains. 12-week trial (n=120) improved testosterone and sexual function. 300-600mg dose. Standardized to 50% Fenuside.
Multiple RCTs. 2025 study: 6-week use improved cognition, working memory, reaction time. 2024 RCT: 21-day use improved deep sleep. Only magnesium form shown to efficiently cross blood-brain barrier.
100+ human clinical studies. RCT for IBS showed significant symptom improvement at 6g/day. Truly regulating fiber - helps both constipation and diarrhea.
RCT (n=50) showed significant hair/nail improvement within 90 days. 500mg daily dose. Solubilized keratin form for bioavailability.
3 human clinical trials with 300+ participants. Fast-acting: results in 5 days at 250mg. Plant-based. Used in Tylenol supplements, Schiff Move Free, Platinum Performance (2025).
Leucine metabolite. Multiple meta-analyses confirm efficacy for muscle preservation, especially in elderly/catabolic states. 3g/day typical dose. Free acid form faster acting.
Patented mango leaf extract (>60% mangiferin). RCTs show cognitive benefits comparable to caffeine without stimulant side effects. Often combined with quercetin for synergy.
Proprietary L. plantarum strain. Multiple RCTs show improved sleep quality, lower morning cortisol, balanced stress response. Part of Probi Mental Health product line.
Branded lutein/zeaxanthin isomer complex with human studies in visual function and eye-strain-related endpoints; many studies are industry sponsored.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Controlled trials of lutein/zeaxanthin isomers report improvements in visual performance and discomfort metrics in screen-use settings.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Purified shilajit ingredient with controlled human data in male reproductive and androgen-related outcomes.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Randomized placebo-controlled male trial reported increases in total and free testosterone over 90 days (PMID: 26395129).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Comparative human pharmacokinetic studies suggest faster uptake versus some standard vitamin C forms; evidence base is narrower than for generic vitamin C.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Clinical crossover data reported higher short-term plasma vitamin C exposure with vitamin C-lipid metabolite formulation (PMID: 18971870).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Consistent evidence for mild-to-moderate depression contexts, but clinically significant CYP/P-gp drug interaction risk requires caution.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Cochrane review (CD000448) reports superiority to placebo in mild-to-moderate depression in many trials with better tolerability than some antidepressants.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Clinical data are broad and endpoint-specific; strongest support is for antioxidant biology rather than universal disease-risk reduction.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: NIH ODS and pooled human trials support antioxidant and deficiency-correction roles with context-dependent clinical outcomes.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Supported in targeted populations and in combination eye-health formulations (e.g., AREDS family formulas).
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: AREDS/AREDS2 programs established role of carotenoid-containing formulas in progression risk reduction for selected AMD populations.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human evidence supports magnesium supplementation for several outcomes in deficiency-risk and condition-specific groups.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Multiple meta-analyses report modest blood-pressure and migraine-prevention benefits, with form- and dose-dependent effects.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Broad human evidence base in bone-health settings, especially when paired with vitamin D and adequate baseline intake assessment.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: NIH ODS and pooled trials support BMD outcomes, with strongest utility in at-risk populations and adequate adherence.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Controlled trials support specific indications such as pregnancy-related nausea and selected PMS outcomes.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Clinical guidance and human trials support B6 in nausea-of-pregnancy protocols and B-complex homocysteine management.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human evidence strongest in deficiency and neuropathy-related contexts; benfotiamine data support symptom-oriented use.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Clinical studies support thiamine repletion and benfotiamine use in neuropathy-related metabolic settings.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human interventional evidence exists but is more limited than major vitamins; strongest support is biochemical and deficiency correction.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Human studies and biochemical evidence support coenzyme A pathway relevance and metabolic support roles.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Best human evidence is in migraine prevention protocols, including clinical guideline support in some regions.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Controlled trials and meta-analyses report reduced migraine frequency with high-dose riboflavin protocols.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human intervention data support blood-pressure effects primarily in low-intake or high-sodium contexts.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Dietary intervention and supplementation studies support BP modulation, with safety constraints in renal impairment.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong deficiency-prevention evidence, especially in pregnancy and low-intake populations; excess iodine risk noted.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: NIH ODS and public-health interventions support iodine's role in thyroid outcomes and developmental health.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human supplementation evidence is more limited than calcium/magnesium but supports deficiency correction and cofactor biology.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Evidence is strongest for essentiality and deficiency correction; interventional outcomes are modest and population-specific.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human evidence supports copper as an essential cofactor with clinically meaningful effects in deficiency states.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: NIH ODS references essentiality and deficiency manifestations; supplementation should avoid excess exposure.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Evidence is primarily essentiality and deficiency correction; supplementation utility is context-specific.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Human evidence supports physiologic necessity; targeted supplementation is generally reserved for low-intake or clinical contexts.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Strong evidence base for LDL-lowering and bowel-regularity outcomes; supports cardiometabolic and GI-health use.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: FDA-authorized CHD claim for soluble fiber from psyllium (21 CFR 101.81) and multiple meta-analyses showing LDL reduction.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Emerging but growing human evidence supports bone and inflammatory biomarker effects at supplemental doses.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Small human trials report favorable shifts in selected inflammatory and hormone-related biomarkers.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human evidence indicates immunomodulatory effects, but heterogeneity and methodological variability are substantial.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Systematic review/meta-analysis of human studies found favorable immune-marker changes with high heterogeneity (PMID: 37952511).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Systematic review evidence suggests modest pain/sinus-related benefits with generally acceptable safety.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Systematic review/meta-analysis of 54 studies found small but significant oral pain benefit and potential sinusitis utility (PMID: 37157782).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human trials and meta-analyses support oral-health applications, with results influenced by extract standardization.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Recent meta-analysis of RCTs found propolis mouthwash reduced plaque/gingival inflammation outcomes (PMID: 41003407).

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Evidence suggests modest, formulation-dependent effects for common-cold outcomes; heterogeneity across preparations is substantial.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Cochrane review (CD000530) and later meta-analyses indicate mixed but signal-positive findings in selected products and regimens.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Systematic-review-level evidence supports honey for acute upper respiratory symptom relief, especially cough severity and duration.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: BMJ Evidence-Based Medicine systematic review (2020/2021) reported better symptom relief with honey vs usual care for URTI symptoms.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Early human and preclinical evidence suggest hormonal-metabolism effects; robust phase-3 style data are limited.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Most evidence remains preclinical or small early-phase human studies.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Generic shilajit evidence is less standardized than purified branded forms; preclinical and early-stage data predominate.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: Mechanistic and animal studies support mitochondrial and antioxidant pathways; composition variability is a major caveat.

backed_clinical_studies.notes: Human RCTs and meta-analyses support pain-reduction outcomes; effect sizes vary by pain indication and study quality.
backed_clinical_studies.notable_studies: 2023 systematic review/meta-analysis of double-blind RCTs (PMID: 36986081) reported significant pain reduction versus comparators.
